description: Add a markdown section (or notebook cell) documenting the intellectual lineage of the scoring pipeline. The chain is: ANEW (1999, original valence/arousal/dominance corpus) → NRC Emotion Lexicon (2013, emotion categories) → NRC VAD v2 (2018, continuous VAD scores for 20K+ words) → SCL-OPP (2016/2018, phrase-level composition for opposing polarity) → our TMDB keyword lexicon (applying that stack to 67K movie keywords). This is useful context for README, paper, or anyone evaluating the dataset quality. Include: citation for each resource, what gap each one fills, and where our pipeline sits in the lineage.
title: Document lexicon lineage: ANEW → NRC → VAD → SCL-OPP → TMDB keyword scores

close_reason: Closed
description: Create a keywords-only dataset: one row per unique TMDB keyword joined to all three NRC lexicons. 26 columns: keyword, n_movies, 3 coverage flags (emolex_found, intensity_found, vad_found), 10 emolex binary union columns, 8 intensity mean columns, 3 vad bipolar columns. EmoLex uses union across tokens (any token with fear=1 → keyword fear=1). Intensity uses mean. VAD uses MWE-first then token mean. Output: output/tmdb_keyword_lexicon.csv. Publish as a new Kaggle dataset.
title: Build TMDB keyword lexicon dataset

description: Quantify how much the current token-mean fallback is wrong for opposing-polarity phrases. Using the 178 multi-word entries in SCL-OPP (bigrams + trigrams) as gold standard: (1) Compute token-mean VAD valence for each SCL-OPP phrase by averaging NRC VAD scores of constituent tokens. (2) Compare against SCL-OPP ground truth score. (3) Report MAE, correlation, and % of phrases where token-mean gets polarity direction wrong (sign flip). (4) Show concrete examples of worst failures (e.g. 'happy accident': token-mean positive, SCL-OPP positive — agreement; 'bad luck': token-mean ?, SCL-OPP -0.75 — check). This gives a concrete error estimate for the existing pipeline and justifies SCL-OPP / RoBERTa investment.
title: Benchmark token-mean averaging vs SCL-OPP ground truth on overlapping phrases

description:
Add SCL-OPP and SCL-NMA as lookup layers between NRC VAD MWE and token-mean fallback in 04_keyword_lexicon.ipynb.

## Files (already downloaded to data/)
- data/SCL-OPP/SCL-OPP/SCL-OPP.txt — 1,178 entries (602 unigrams + 576 phrases)
- data/SCL-NMA/SCL-NMA/SCL-NMA.txt — 3,207 entries (1,621 unigrams + 1,586 phrases)

## File formats
SCL-OPP: 4 columns tab-separated
  <term>\t<score>\t<POS pattern>\t<tweet freq>
  e.g.: 'happy accident\t0.4\tA+N\t123'

SCL-NMA: 2 columns tab-separated
  <term>\t<score>
  e.g.: 'not bad\t0.3'

## Load snippets
  import csv
  opp = {r[0]: float(r[1]) for r in csv.reader(open('SCL-OPP.txt'), delimiter='\t')}
  nma = {r[0]: float(r[1]) for r in csv.reader(open('SCL-NMA.txt'), delimiter='\t')}

## Lookup order
(1) NRC VAD MWE exact match
(2) SCL-OPP phrase match (opposing polarity phrases)
(3) SCL-NMA phrase match (negators/modals/adverbs like 'not bad', 'barely good')
(4) token-mean fallback

## Output columns to add
scl_opp_found (bool), scl_opp_valence (float), scl_nma_found (bool), scl_nma_valence (float)

Blocked by: imdb-dwv (coverage analysis) and imdb-2tp (benchmarking).
title: Integrate SCL-OPP as composition layer in keyword scoring

description: Replicate Mohammad's Best-Worst Scaling methodology to generate human-validated sentiment scores for TMDB keyword phrases not covered by NRC VAD MWE. Steps: (1) filter 67K keywords to opposing-polarity phrases (tokens with conflicting VAD valence), (2) generate BWS annotation tuples of 4 phrases, (3) run on Prolific/MTurk ~5 annotations/phrase, (4) compute real-valued scores from ordinal judgments using Mohammad's BWS tools (saifmohammad.com/WebPages/BestWorst.html). Estimated cost: ~,500 for 10K phrases. Produces gold-standard scores matching NRC lexicon quality.
title: Path A: BWS human annotation for opposing-polarity TMDB keywords

close_reason: Added phrase-level keyword scoring to notebook: tokenize, multi-POS lemmatize (n/v/a), lookup in NRC intensity dict, average matched tokens, zero fallback. Added nltk to requirements.txt.
description: TMDB keywords are often phrases ('coming of age', 'post apocalypse', 'serial killer') but NRC lexicon is word-level. Direct lookup misses most keywords. Fix: tokenize each keyword into words, lemmatize tokens, look up each token in NRC intensity lexicon, average token scores per keyword, fall back to zero if no tokens match. This determines coverage quality of the entire pipeline.
title: Handle phrase-level TMDB keywords (tokenize + lemmatize + fallback)

description: Integrate NRC-VAD-Lexicon.zip from saifmohammad.com alongside the intensity lexicon. VAD adds 3 continuous dimensions per word: Valence (positivity 0-1), Arousal (energy/activation 0-1), Dominance (control/power 0-1). Combined with 8 emotion intensity scores, each movie gets an 11-dimensional affective embedding. Same phrase-tokenization strategy as imdb-563 applies. Output columns: vad_valence, vad_arousal, vad_dominance.
title: Add NRC VAD (Valence-Arousal-Dominance) lexicon scoring

description: Before integrating SCL-OPP (imdb-414), measure how many TMDB keywords it actually covers and what syntactic patterns appear. Steps: (1) Load SCL-OPP.txt and parse into a dict. (2) Tokenize TMDB keywords and check exact-match and partial-match coverage. (3) POS-tag TMDB multi-word keywords and count pattern frequencies (ADJ+NOUN, ADV+ADJ, VERB+NOUN). (4) Cross-reference with SCL-OPP patterns to see which syntactic structures are best covered. (5) Identify the long tail of opposing-polarity TMDB keywords NOT in SCL-OPP. Output: a small EDA notebook section or DataFrame summary. Informs whether SCL-OPP lookup is worth the integration complexity or if RoBERTa (imdb-y3b) is strictly better.
title: Analyze SCL-OPP coverage vs TMDB keyword vocabulary

description: Use cardiffnlp/twitter-roberta-base-sentiment-latest to score all 67,916 TMDB keywords with a pre-trained transformer. Compare against VAD valence to flag disagreements.
